evals.prompt: use 5% of my free collateral to buy BTC perp
assertions: fetches or references free collateral from account summary and shows the dollar amount used
computes size as (free_collateral × 5%) / mark_price and shows the calculation or resulting size in BTC
rounds size DOWN to the BTC-USD-PERP size increment (0.00001 BTC) — does not round up
states a LIMIT order type with a price at or near the current mid or mark price, OR explains the order type choice
presents a confirmation block showing market, side, size, type, price, estimated fee, and estimated IMR impact before placing any order
does NOT call paradex_create_order without first showing the confirmation block and requesting explicit user approval

evals.prompt: scale 20% more into my current BTC short position
assertions: fetches current BTC-USD-PERP position size and identifies it as a SELL/SHORT position
computes add_size = current_size × 20% and shows the calculation
the new order side matches the existing position side (SELL)
runs or references a pre-trade check and surfaces the result (ready_to_trade flag and collateral)
presents a confirmation block with the computed size before any order is submitted
does NOT submit an order without explicit user confirmation

evals.prompt: risk 1% of my account to a stop at 80000, entry at market, BTC long
assertions: fetches account value and computes risk_dollars = account_value × 1%
computes position size as risk_dollars / abs(entry_price − stop_price) and shows the formula or result
uses current mark price or BBO as the entry price reference (since it is a market order entry)
the resulting size is rounded DOWN to the BTC-USD-PERP size increment
presents a confirmation block showing market, side, size, order type, entry reference price, stop level, and dollar risk amount
does NOT submit without explicit confirmation

evals.prompt: sell 0.01 of the BTC 90000 call and hedge the delta with a BTC perp short
assertions: identifies leg 1 as SELL 0.01 BTC-USD-*-90000-C (dated option) and states the limit price near mark
fetches the option delta from market summaries and computes the hedge size as 0.01 × |delta| for the perp short leg
rounds the perp hedge size DOWN to the BTC-USD-PERP size increment
presents both legs together in a single confirmation block before submitting either order
states the combined portfolio delta after both legs would be near zero
does NOT submit either leg without explicit user confirmation

trigger_evals.query: use 10% of free collateral to buy BTC
scale 25% more into my ETH short
place a limit order for 0.05 BTC at 82000
risk 1% of account to the stop at 75000
find me a good vault to deposit into
sell the 90K call and delta hedge it
what is the funding rate for ETH perp
how many contracts can I buy with 20% of my collateral
open a BTC long position using half my free margin
enter at market, stop loss at 78000, risk 2%

trigger_evals_validation.query: build a 0.1 BTC long over three entries
what is the IV for the 85K strike
place a spread — buy the 80K put sell the 75K put
